title: Earthquake Safety and Emergency Response Bond
subject: General-obligation bond for earthquake, fire, and disaster preparedness.
plainSummary: Proposition A authorizes a $535 million general-obligation bond to seismically retrofit and rebuild San Francisco's critical emergency-response and transit infrastructure. The money is allocated up to: $200M to replace the 110-year-old Potrero bus yard with a seismically safe Muni facility, $130M to expand the high-pressure Emergency Firefighting Water System (cisterns, pipes, tunnels), $100M to retrofit or replace neighborhood fire stations, $72M to retrofit or replace neighborhood police stations, and $33M for other public-safety facility repairs. As a general-obligation bond it requires a two-thirds (66.67%) supermajority to pass. The city pays it back through existing property taxes and pledges not to raise property-tax rates above San Francisco's charter-set limit (debt is issued as older bonds retire), so it is described as funding major infrastructure without a tax-rate increase. Total repayment is estimated at roughly $933 million over 25 years. SPUR endorsed YES, arguing the seismic resilience of first-responder infrastructure deserves continued public investment. A grassroots opposition (led by John Crabtree, 'No on A') argues it is a 'boondoggle' that wrongly diverts earthquake-safety dollars to a Muni bus yard and prefers extending the saltwater hydrant system; no financed opposition committee registered against the measure.

yesPositions.rationale: YES authorizes new city debt (a $535M general-obligation bond) to fund major public-safety infrastructure.

support.committee: Yes on A, San Franciscans for Fire, Earthquake, and Disaster Preparedness, sponsored by San Francisco Firefighters local 798

funding.notes: Support total = monetary contributions (FPPC Form 460 Line 1, cumulative) for the registered 'Yes on A' committee (filer_nid 215726436); cross-verified by summing the committee's 90 itemized Schedule A transactions, which equal $1,633,500 exactly. No financed opposition committee registered against the earthquake bond in DataSF (the only active 'No on A' committee, filer_nid 212872841, is 'No on A, Stop the Engardio Recall' — an unrelated measure on a different ballot). Opposition to Prop A was grassroots/unfinanced (label: oppose funding unavailable / $0 reported).
